summary: Iteration 5 (friendlier rebuild of LandingPage + Dashboard, keeping AIOrb soul) verified end-to-end on the public surface. Backend 4/4 PASS (root, health, config, 404). Frontend webpack compiles clean ('No issues found.'). At 1440x900 desktop: H1 'Your HR questions, answered in seconds.' renders with gradient on the second line, .ai-orb-core is present (1), the 'Sample reply' card under the orb contains 'India Leave Policy v3.2' with citation 'India-Leave-v3.2.pdf · §4.1 · p.12', 'Three steps to an answer.' renders with cards 01/02/03, 'Designed for daily HR life.' renders with the 'A chat that knows HR' bento card, bottom CTA '60 seconds to your first answer.' renders with data-testid=cta-bottom-signup. At 390x844 mobile: document.documentElement.scrollWidth == 390 (NO horizontal overflow), H1 still intact. /app/dashboard while unauthenticated correctly redirects to /sign-in. /this-does-not-exist shows the '404 / We lost this page' page. /sign-in still renders ('Every policy. Every region. One assistant.'). Visual screenshots confirm the page is rendered DARK (near-black background, AIOrb glowing). ZERO console errors captured during the entire walkthrough.

issues: document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') returns False on first JS eval after navigation, even though the page IS visually dark (MeshBackground paints near-black, gradient text reads correctly). Same finding as iteration 4 — LandingPage uses useEffect (post-paint) to add the 'dark' class. If any descendant ever relies on Tailwind 'dark:' utilities, there would be a brief light flash on first paint. Suggest moving to useLayoutEffect or setting the class synchronously in index.html. No user-visible impact today.

action_items: (Optional / cosmetic) Replace useEffect with useLayoutEffect in LandingPage.tsx for the 'dark' class toggle, or set it synchronously in index.html. Today the visual is dark (verified in screenshots) so this is non-blocking.
Chat AI remains MOCKED (no n8n webhook). When the n8n webhook is wired, run an authenticated walkthrough through CommandSpotlight → /app/chat?q=... to verify the streaming reply.

Authenticated Employee Dashboard.tsx (the second file rewritten this iteration) could not be exercised without seeded Supabase users. Once a seeded employee credential is available, drive a logged-in test to verify: 180px AIOrb on the left, headline 'How can I help today?', CommandSpotlight, 4 stat cards with sublabels, 4 categorized suggestion tabs (Time off / Pay & benefits / Policies / Career) with 3 sample questions each, 5 quick links, 'Pick up where you left off' conversations list, and 'What's new' alerts panel.
critical_code_review_comments: LandingPage.tsx (231 lines) is fine in size and well-structured. The dark-class side effect should be useLayoutEffect to avoid a theoretical first-paint flash; not a real bug today because the page uses explicit text-white + dark inline styles and MeshBackground is opaque-dark.
AIOrb (unchanged) still attaches a global mousemove listener — flagged in iter 4. Consider rAF throttling + matchMedia('(pointer: fine)') gating on touch devices.

FeatureCard subcomponent declared in the same file is fine, but its outer className contains 'md:col-span-2' hard-coded — if you ever drop a FeatureCard outside the 6-col bento it will misalign. Pass span as a prop or let the parent set it.
Backend remains 3 endpoints + 404 catch-all — no new review notes.
Chat backend integration is still MOCKED (no n8n webhook). Not a code-quality issue, just a reminder that the headline value prop ('answers in seconds, cited') is currently fronted by a mock.

success_rate.frontend: 12/13 functional checks PASS. The 1 'fail' is dark-class-via-useEffect which is cosmetic only — visual dark theme is verified in screenshots.

test_credentials: /app/memory/test_credentials.md not present. Not required this iteration — only public pages, route guard, 404, and backend health were in scope.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 friendlier rebuild verified. All 14 required checks from the review request pass except the JS-side 'document.documentElement.classList.contains(dark)' eval which returns False (timing — useEffect runs post-paint). Visually the page IS DARK (confirmed in screenshots). Backend 4/4 PASS. Mobile 390x844 has NO horizontal overflow. /app/dashboard guard redirect to /sign-in works. /this-does-not-exist returns the 404 'We lost this page' screen. Sign-in renders unchanged. The new Dashboard.tsx (friendlier daily-use surface with 180px AIOrb, categorized suggestion tabs, 4 stat cards, 5 quick links, conversations list, alerts panel) is NOT testable without seeded Supabase users — request the main agent to seed an employee account and provide credentials so the next iteration can exercise it. Chat remains MOCKED until n8n webhook is plugged in.
rca of the issue: Single cosmetic finding: 'dark' class on <html> is applied via useEffect (post-paint) and therefore not present on the very first JS read after navigation. RCA: useEffect fires after the browser paints. Mitigation: switch to useLayoutEffect or set the class synchronously in public/index.html. Reproduction: navigate to /, immediately page.evaluate('document.documentElement.classList.contains("dark")') → false; wait 200ms and re-evaluate → true. No user-visible impact because MeshBackground + explicit text-white classes already render the page dark on first paint.
